import collections
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

def task_func(data_dict):
    if not data_dict:
        return None, "The distribution is uniform."

    categories = list(data_dict.keys())
    counts = list(data_dict.values())
    avg_count = sum(counts) / len(categories)

    for count in counts:
        if np.abs(count - avg_count) > 1e-5:
            break
    else:
        uniformity_message = "The distribution is uniform."

    if uniformity_message == "The distribution is uniform.":
        bins = min(len(categories), 10)
    else:
        bins = np.linspace(min(counts), max(counts), num=10)

    plt.bar(categories, counts, width=0.8, align='center')
    plt.xticks(np.arange(len(categories)), categories)
    plt.xlim([-1, len(categories)+1])
    hist_axes = plt.gca()
    plt.title(uniformity_message)
    plt.tight_layout()

    return hist_axes, uniformity_message
